etymology.description: semantic 雈 ('owl') + phonetic 臼 (sound) -> originally a type of owl -> borrowed for 'old'
etymology.interpretation: 舊 originally depicted a type of owl, using 雈 for its meaning. It was later borrowed phonetically to represent the concept 'old', as the sound of the character matched a word for 'old' in phonetic series.
etymology.simplification: 舊(舊) was simplified to 旧(舊) by removing 雈 (owl) and replacing it with the simpler component 臼 (used as a radical and phonetic).

examples.chinese: 这本书很旧(這本書很舊)。
我找到了旧照片(我找到了舊照片)。
他老想着过去的旧时光(他老想着過去的舊時光)。
examples.pinyin: Tā lǎo xiǎngzhe guòqù de jiù shíguāng.
examples.english: He always reminisces about the past times.
